chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Cr. Bail Application No.279 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs For hearing of bail application -------------------------------------------- 27.02.2025 Mr. Ghulam Sarwar Lakho, advocate for applicant Ms. Seema Zaidi, Additional Prosecutor General Sindh Complainant present ------------------------------------------- Applicants/accused Hussain Parhiyar, Ukeer Ahmed Parhiyar, Dhani Bux Parihyar and Muhammad Juman Parhiyar seek pre-arrest bail in FIR No.02/2025, registered at P.S. Mirpur Bathoro, District Sujawal for offence under sections 379, 427, 34, PPC, after rejection of their bail plea by learned Additional Sessions Judge, Sujawal vide order dated 22.01.2025. 2. Brief facts of the case are that there is dispute between complainant and present applicants over survey Nos.118, 119 and 127, Deh Samani. On 25.12.2024, complainant and his relatives Abdul Aziz were at home when his relative Abdul Rasheed came and informed him that Hussain, Ukeer, Dhani Bux and Juman, duly armed, came to complainant’s land, cut down acacia trees and taken away the same on donkey cart. Complainant rushed to his lands and saw that 15 to 20 trees of acacia were lying there. Hence the subject FIR. 3. Learned counsel for applicants/accused argued that there is civil dispute between the parties over the agricultural land; that litigation is pending between the parties and brother of complainant was convicted in murder case, lodged by applicants side against complainant of this case; that they are close relatives and the alleged incident had taken place but the complainant has managed false FIR against the applicants/accused. 4.

Hence the subject FIR. 3. Learned counsel for applicants/accused argued that there is civil dispute between the parties over the agricultural land; that litigation is pending between the parties and brother of complainant was convicted in murder case, lodged by applicants side against complainant of this case; that they are close relatives and the alleged incident had taken place but the complainant has managed false FIR against the applicants/accused. 4. Complainant is present in person and submits that accused persons forcefully occupied his land, however, he admits that no civil litigation is pending between the parties. He further submits that they are close relatives and main applicant/accused Hussain Parhiyar is his cousin. 5. Learned Additional Prosecutor General Sindh has recorded no objection for grant of bail to the applicants/ accused on the ground that there is civil dispute between the parties. 6. Heard learned counsel for applicants/accused, Additional Prosecutor General Sindh, complainant in person and perused the material available on record. 7. From tentative assessment of material available on record, it appears that there is civil dispute between the parties over agricultural land. Allegation against applicants/ accused is that they have occupied the land of complainant, cut down acacia trees from the land and taken away the same. Complainant himself admits that applicants’ party is in occupation of subject land since 2019 but no civil litigation is pending between the parties. All these facts make out a case of further inquiry in terms of Section 497, Cr.PC. It is well settled law that merits cannot be considered at bail stage and only tentative assessment can be made, as held by apex Court in case of Zeeshan versus the State and another (2024 SCMR 1716). 8. In view of above, interim pre-arrest bail granted to applicants Hussain Parhiyar, Ukeer Ahmed Parhiyar, Dhani Bux Parihyar and Muhammad Juman Parhiyar by this Court vide order dated 30.01.2025 in the aforesaid FIR is hereby confirmed on the same terms and conditions. 9. The instant bail application is accordingly disposed of.

In view of above, interim pre-arrest bail granted to applicants Hussain Parhiyar, Ukeer Ahmed Parhiyar, Dhani Bux Parihyar and Muhammad Juman Parhiyar by this Court vide order dated 30.01.2025 in the aforesaid FIR is hereby confirmed on the same terms and conditions. 9. The instant bail application is accordingly disposed of. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
